title: Magnificently Crunchy Japanese Apple Tamagoyaki
description: A paleo-friendly Japanese dish featuring apples and render-fat, perfect for a crunchy snack. This tamagoyaki is a funky twist on traditional Japanese cuisine.
instructions: Preheat a non-stick tamagoyaki pan over medium heat.
In a large bowl, whisk together eggs, salt, and black pepper until well combined.
Add diced apples, coconut flour, and render-fat to the bowl and mix until a thick batter forms.
Brush the preheated pan with sesame oil and pour in about 1/4 of the egg mixture.
Tilt the pan to evenly coat the bottom and cook until the edges start to set.
Use a spatula to gently lift and fold the edges of the tamagoyaki towards the center.
Repeat the process with the remaining egg mixture, creating multiple layers.
Once the tamagoyaki is cooked through, remove it from the pan and let it cool on a wire rack.
Slice the tamagoyaki into thick rounds and garnish with chopped scallions.
Serve immediately and enjoy the crunchy texture and sweet apple flavor.
